pattern: Tracking coverage as a sorted set of disjoint half-open intervals lets add, remove, and query each be done by locating the affected span and splicing only the boundaries that change.
hints: Keep the tracked region as a list of non-overlapping intervals sorted by start; a number is covered exactly when it falls inside one of them.
Adding an interval merges it with every interval it touches or overlaps into one larger interval; removing carves a hole that may split an interval in two.
A query is true only if a single tracked interval starts at or before the left bound and ends at or after the right bound, since the intervals never overlap.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: add, punch a hole, then three queries straddling the gap
query before anything is added is false
half-open: querying up to the exact end is covered, but one past it is not
two abutting added ranges fuse so a query across the seam is true
removing the middle of a range splits it, leaving the ends tracked
overlapping adds extend coverage without leaving gaps
remove that spans multiple ranges clears all of them

stress.description: many random add/remove/query operations over a small coordinate space so overlaps, splits, and merges happen densely
stress.note: tight coordinate range forces frequent boundary splicing and coalescing

complexity.time: O(n) per add/remove worst case, O(log n) per query
